_comment: All pixel coordinates are based on a 1920x1080 capture. If your capture resolution differs, set base_width/base_height to match how these were measured (1920x1080) and the tool scales automatically. Tune regions with calibrate mode.
_capture_help: Live capture. mode 'obs' = read the OBS Virtual Camera instead of capturing the screen (ZERO game overhead, no stutter, keeps exclusive fullscreen - requires OBS running with a Game Capture of Apex and Virtual Camera started; see OBS setup below). mode 'monitor' = Windows Graphics Capture of the whole monitor Apex is on (works without OBS but an exclusive-fullscreen game stutters while capturing). mode 'window' = WGC of just the Apex window. monitor_index is the fallback monitor for WGC modes if Apex isn't found. throttle_ms caps how often WGC grabs a frame (WGC modes only).
_obs_setup_help: For mode 'obs': in OBS add a Source -> Game Capture -> capture Apex (mode 'Capture specific window' = r5apex), then click 'Start Virtual Camera' in OBS Controls. The tracker auto-finds the device by name (video_device_name). If auto-detect picks the wrong camera, set video_device_index to the device's index (run 'py apex_tracker.py devices' to list them). The OBS scene must show the full end screen un-cropped and with no overlays covering the player cards / placement / kills / bottom-left id.

capture._video_res_help: Resolution to request from the OBS Virtual Camera (mode 'obs'). Match your OBS Output (Scaled) Resolution - Settings > Video. Without this the camera defaults to 640x480, too small for the OCR. Use 1920x1080 (default) or 2560x1440 etc.
capture._on_demand_help: BETA / OPT-IN, default false. true = capture only in short bursts (open capture, grab a frame to check for the end screen, close it, then idle) so an EXCLUSIVE-FULLSCREEN game keeps its fast present path during play. WARNING: it only glances at the screen once every idle_probe_seconds, so it can MISS the end screen and fail to log a match (this is why it is not the default). For a stutter-free AND reliable setup, use mode 'obs' instead. Leave this false unless you've tested that it reliably logs your matches.
capture._debug_log_help: true = append timestamped lines to debug/tracker_debug.log for every capture probe (when it opens capture, what it saw, when it idled). Use it to line probes up against any stutter you felt in-game. Set false once tuned.

_low_priority_help: Run the tracker at below-normal process priority so Windows favors Apex. Recommended true.
_settle_help: Seconds to wait after the end-screen banner appears before reading it, so placement/stats are fully drawn (prevents blank/partial rows).
_heartbeat_help: Seconds between 'still watching' status lines so you can tell it's alive. Set 0 to disable.
_new_file_each_run_help: false = one running file that accumulates every match (default). true = a new timestamped file each time you start 'watch', e.g. apex_matches_20260604_193512.csv.
_known_names_help: Recurring squadmate gamertags. OCR names are snapped to the closest match here (fixes stray glyphs like WhopperGob->WhopperGot). Add your squad's names.
_resolution_help: The regions below are measured at base_width x base_height (1920x1080) and auto-scale to whatever resolution is captured, which already covers any 16:9 res (1440p, 4K). force_resolution pins a profile key like '2560x1440' (run 'setup' to set it interactively, or pass --res); leave it empty to auto-detect each run. profiles holds per-resolution region sets for cases where pure scaling is slightly off - each profile carries its own base_width/base_height plus header/columns/rows/detect blocks measured at that native resolution, and is used as-is (no scaling) when its key matches. See CALIBRATION.md.

1920x1080-native-16x9._comment: For players running Apex at NATIVE 16:9 (not the 16:10-squished-into-16:9 setup the base regions are tuned for). Same 1920x1080 capture size, but the summary screen spreads wider horizontally and taller vertically, so the cards/header/banner sit differently. Select this profile (force_resolution or the GUI resolution dropdown) if you run native 16:9 and the base regions miss.

_dashboard_url_help: Optional. URL of your online stats dashboard; the UI's Dashboard button opens it. Leave empty and the button will prompt to set one.

detect._comment: Banner region, used ONLY to recognize the end-of-match summary screen (win = gold CHAMPIONS, any other placement = red SQUAD ELIMINATED). The banner text itself is not stored. A cheap gold/red color check gates a confirming OCR, so OCR does not run during gameplay.
